explicit_text: I was living in Chicago at 64th and California when I entered the service in August 1944. Growing up on the south side of Chicago shaped much of my early life, including attending Lindblom High School. Chicago was home during my formative years and the place where my family lived while I prepared to join the military.

explicit_text: I graduated from Lindblom High School in Chicago in 1944. While I was there, they had an ROTC program, which I joined with the hope of becoming a commissioned officer. Lindblom was where I finished my formal education before entering the Navy.

explicit_text: I remember hearing about the attack on Pearl Harbor on December 7, 1941, while at a temple bazaar with my parents. It was a pivotal moment that changed everything, and people thought the war would be over quickly. Pearl Harbor was the event that really brought the war home to us.

explicit_text: I joined the Boy Scouts when I was 12, although the troop I joined folded after about six months. Still, I was interested enough to continue on a provisional basis and eventually earned my Eagle Scout. Scouting taught me discipline and skills that helped later in the military.

explicit_text: Alan Milach was a friend of mine who was ahead of me in school and qualified for the Army A-12 military program. He urged me to join the Navy before I was drafted, advice that ultimately saved me from being in a program that was later discontinued. We kept in touch by mail while he was at Hunter College in New York City.

explicit_text: I enlisted in the United States Navy in August 1944, despite having asthma as a child, which I did not disclose. The Navy took me through boot camp and service school at Great Lakes, and I trained as a Quartermaster. Serving in the Navy was my way to contribute to the war effort in the Pacific.

explicit_text: I went through boot camp and service school at the Great Lakes Naval Training Station near Chicago. There, I had a one-on-one interview to determine my Navy rating and was assigned Quartermaster as my second choice. Great Lakes was also where I recovered from scarlet fever during an epidemic.
